title: HIGH: (request-target) not required in inbox signature verification
description:
In src/server.rs handle_inbox(), signed_header_names is checked for 'digest' and 'date' (lines 726-733), but neither '(request-target)' nor 'host' is required. An attacker who obtains a valid signed request can replay it against a different path or host.

The server-side verify_signature() function in signatures.rs correctly handles (request-target) when it IS listed in the headers field -- it reconstructs the signed string with the actual request method and path. But because the inbox handler doesn't REQUIRE it, an attacker can craft a Signature header with headers="date digest" (omitting (request-target) and host) and the signature will cover only the date and digest -- not the request path or target host.

Attack scenarios:
1. Cross-inbox replay: A validly-signed request to /users/alice/inbox can be replayed to /users/bob/inbox or /inbox. The actor-mismatch check (line 846) mitigates Follow abuse, but Undo{Follow} replayed to the shared inbox could target a different persona.
2. Cross-host replay: Without 'host' in signed headers, a signature valid for one server can be replayed against another broadside instance (or any server accepting the same signature format).
3. Method confusion (theoretical): Without (request-target), the signature doesn't bind the HTTP method.

Mastodon requires all four: (request-target), host, date, digest.

Fix: Add checks requiring '(request-target)' and 'host' in signed_header_names, alongside the existing digest and date checks (lines 726-733 in server.rs).
close_reason: Added (request-target) and host to required signed headers in handle_inbox (server.rs). Now requires all four: (request-target), host, digest, date — matching Mastodon.
